briefdescription.para: Touch signal type @SINCE_1_1.37.

Wheel signal type @SINCE_1_0.0.

Creates an uninitialized Actor; this can be initialized with Actor::New().

detaileddescription.para: Calling member functions with an uninitialized Actor handle is not allowed. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Dali::Actor is intended as a base class.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Copy constructor.
Assignment operator.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
The actor to copy
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Move constructor.
Move assignment operator.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.22
rhs
A reference to the actor to move
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Gets the layer in which the actor is present.
Adds a child Actor to this Actor.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
child
The child
This Actor (the parent) has been initialized.
The child actor has been initialized.
The child actor is not the same as the parent actor.
The actor is not the Root actor.
The child will be referenced by its parent. This means that the child will be kept alive, even if the handle passed into this method is reset or destroyed.
If the child already has a parent, it will be removed from old parent and reparented to this actor. This may change child's position, color, scale etc as it now inherits them from this actor.

briefdescription.para: Removes a child Actor from this Actor.

detaileddescription.para:
If the actor was not a child of this actor, this is a no-op. @SINCE_1_0.0
child
The child
This Actor (the parent) has been initialized.
The child actor is not the same as the parent actor.

briefdescription.para: Removes an actor from its parent.

detaileddescription.para:
If the actor has no parent, this method does nothing. @SINCE_1_0.0
The (child) actor has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the number of children held by the actor.
Retrieve and child actor by index.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
index
The index of the child to retrieve
The actor for the given index or empty handle if children not initialized
The Actor has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Search through this actor's hierarchy for an actor with the given name.

detaileddescription.para:
The actor itself is also considered in the search. @SINCE_2_0.9
actorName
The name of the actor to find
A handle to the actor if found, or an empty handle if not
The Actor has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Search through this actor's hierarchy for an actor with the given unique ID.

detaileddescription.para:
The actor itself is also considered in the search. @SINCE_1_0.0
id
The ID of the actor to find
A handle to the actor if found, or an empty handle if not
The Actor has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the actor's parent.
Retrieves the actor's size.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0 
The actor's target size 


The actor has been initialized. 


This return is the value that was set using SetSize or the target size of an animation. It may not match the current value in some cases, i.e. when the animation is progressing or the maximum or minimum size is set.

briefdescription.para: Returns the natural size of the actor.

detaileddescription.para: Deriving classes stipulate the natural size and by default an actor has a ZERO natural size.

@SINCE_1_0.0
The actor's natural size

briefdescription.para: Translates an actor relative to its existing position.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
distance
The actor will move by this distance
The actor has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Applies a relative rotation to an actor.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
angle
The angle to the rotation to combine with the existing orientation
axis
The axis of the rotation to combine with the existing orientation
The actor has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Applies a relative rotation to an actor.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
angle
The angle to the rotation to combine with the existing orientation
axis
The axis of the rotation to combine with the existing orientation
The actor has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Applies a relative rotation to an actor.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
relativeRotation
The rotation to combine with the existing orientation
The actor has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Applies a relative scale to an actor.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
relativeScale
The scale to combine with the actor's existing scale
The actor has been initialized.

memberdef.argsstring: (float &localX, float &localY, float screenX, float screenY) const
memberdef.name: ScreenToLocal

briefdescription.para: Converts screen coordinates into the actor's coordinate system using the default camera.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
localX
On return, the X-coordinate relative to the actor
localY
On return, the Y-coordinate relative to the actor
screenX
The screen X-coordinate
screenY
The screen Y-coordinate
True if the conversion succeeded
The Actor has been initialized.
The actor coordinates are relative to the top-left (0.0, 0.0, 0.5)

briefdescription.para: Raise actor above the next sibling actor.
Lower the actor below the previous sibling actor.
Raise actor above all other sibling actors.
Lower actor to the bottom of all other sibling actors.
Raises the actor above the target actor.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_2.60
target
The target actor
The Actor has been initialized.
The Actor has been parented.
The target actor is a sibling.

briefdescription.para: Lower the actor to below the target actor.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_2.60
target
The target actor
The Actor has been initialized.
The Actor has been parented.
The target actor is a sibling.

briefdescription.para: Sets the resize policy to be used for the given dimension(s).

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
policy
The resize policy to use
dimension
The dimension(s) to set policy for. Can be a bitfield of multiple dimensions

briefdescription.para: Returns the resize policy used for a single dimension.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
dimension
The dimension to get policy for
Return the dimension resize policy. If more than one dimension is requested, just return the first one found

briefdescription.para: Calculates the height of the actor given a width.

detaileddescription.para: The natural size is used for default calculation. size 0 is treated as aspect ratio 1:1.

@SINCE_1_0.0
width
Width to use
Return the height based on the width

briefdescription.para: Calculates the width of the actor given a height.

detaileddescription.para: The natural size is used for default calculation. size 0 is treated as aspect ratio 1:1.

@SINCE_1_0.0
height
Height to use
Return the width based on the height

briefdescription.para: Returns the value of negotiated dimension for the given dimension.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
dimension
The dimension to retrieve
Return the value of the negotiated dimension. If more than one dimension is requested, just return the first one found

briefdescription.para: Sets a flag to identify whether the Actor is ignored or not. If the actor is marked as ignored, it will not be rendered and will be excluded from render thread computation. So, the current properties like WorldPosition and WorldColor become inaccurate.
Returns whether the Actor is ignored or not.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_4.21
Return True if the Actor is ignored.

briefdescription.para: Adds a renderer to this actor.

detaileddescription.para:
We don't allow to add duplicated renderers. If we add the same renderer twice, it will just return the index of renderer.
@SINCE_1_0.0
renderer
Renderer to add to the actor
The index of the Renderer that was added
The renderer must be initialized.

briefdescription.para: Gets the number of renderers on this actor.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The number of renderers on this actor

briefdescription.para: Gets a Renderer by index.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
index
The index of the renderer to fetch
The renderer at the specified index
The index must be between 0 and GetRendererCount()-1

briefdescription.para: Removes a renderer from the actor.
Removes a renderer from the actor by index.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
index
Index of the renderer that is to be removed
The index must be between 0 and GetRendererCount()-1

briefdescription.para: Add renderer drawing cached output to this Actor. @SINCE_2_3.54.

parameterdescription.para: Renderer to set to the Actor

simplesect.para: The index of the Renderer that was added

briefdescription.para: Get total number of cache renderers. @SINCE_2_4.19.

simplesect.para: The size of cache renderer container

briefdescription.para: Removes cache renderer from the Actor.
This signal is emitted when touch input is received.

detaileddescription.para:
A callback of the following type may be connected: boolYourCallbackName(Actoractor,TouchEvent&touch);
 The return value of True, indicates that the touch event has been consumed. Otherwise the signal will be emitted on the next sensitive parent of the actor. @SINCE_1_9.28 
The signal to connect to 


The Actor has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when hover input is received.

detaileddescription.para:
A callback of the following type may be connected: boolYourCallbackName(Actoractor,constHoverEvent&event);
 The return value of True, indicates that the hover event should be consumed. Otherwise the signal will be emitted on the next sensitive parent of the actor. @SINCE_1_0.0 
The signal to connect to 


The Actor has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when wheel event is received.

detaileddescription.para:
A callback of the following type may be connected: boolYourCallbackName(Actoractor,constWheelEvent&event);
 The return value of True, indicates that the wheel event should be consumed. Otherwise the signal will be emitted on the next sensitive parent of the actor. @SINCE_1_0.0 
The signal to connect to 


The Actor has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted after the actor has been connected to the scene.

detaileddescription.para:
When an actor is connected, it will be directly or indirectly parented to the root Actor. @SINCE_1_9.24
The signal to connect to
The root Actor is provided automatically by the Scene, and is always considered to be connected.
When the parent of a set of actors is connected to the stage, then all of the children will received this callback. For the following actor tree, the callback order will be A, B, D, E, C, and finally F.
A(parent) /\ BC /\\ DEF

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted after the actor has been disconnected from the scene.

detaileddescription.para: If an actor is disconnected it either has no parent, or is parented to a disconnected actor.

@SINCE_1_9.24
The signal to connect to
When the parent of a set of actors is disconnected to the scene, then all of the children will received this callback, starting with the leaf actors. For the following actor tree, the callback order will be D, E, B, F, C, and finally A.
A(parent) /\ BC /\\ DEF

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted after the size has been set on the actor during relayout.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The signal

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the layout direction property of this or a parent actor is changed.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Actoractor,LayoutDirection::Typetype); actor: The actor, or child of actor, whose layout direction has changed type: Whether the actor's layout direction property has changed or a parent's.

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the visible property of this actor or any of its parents (right up to the root layer) changes.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Actoractor,boolvisible); actor: The actor whose inherited visibility has changed. visible: This is true if this actor's inherited VISIBLE property is true. If it is true, it denotes one of the 2 cases. One is VISIBLE property of this actor or only one of the parent actors were originally false and it becomes true now. Another is this actor is connected on Scene now with that the VISIBLE property of this actor and all of its parent were true. If it is false, it also denotes one of the 2 cases. One is that VISIBLE property of this actor and all of the parent actors were originally true but one of them becomes false now. Another is VISIBLE property of this actor and all of the parent actors are true and this actor is disconnected from the Scene now.

@SINCE_2_3.22
The signal to connect to
The Actor has been initialized.
This signal is NOT emitted if the actor becomes transparent (or the reverse).
For reference, an actor is only shown if it and it's parents (up to the root actor) are also visible, are not transparent, and this actor has a non-zero size.

briefdescription.para: Creates an initialized Actor.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
A handle to a newly allocated Dali resource

briefdescription.para: Downcasts a handle to Actor handle.

detaileddescription.para: If handle points to an Actor object, the downcast produces valid handle. If not, the returned handle is left uninitialized.

@SINCE_1_0.0
handle
to An object
handle to a Actor object or an uninitialized handle

briefdescription.para: Actor is the primary object with which Dali applications interact.

detaileddescription.para: UI controls can be built by combining multiple actors.
Touch or hover events are received via signals; see Actor::TouchedSignal() and Actor::HoveredSignal() for more details.

listitem.para: An actor is only hittable if the actor's touch or hover signal has a connection.
An actor is only hittable when it is between the camera's near and far planes.
If an actor is made insensitive, then the actor and its children are not hittable; see Actor::Property::SENSITIVE.
If an actor's visibility flag is unset, then none of its children are hittable either; see Actor::Property::VISIBLE.
To be hittable, an actor must have a non-zero size.
If an actor's world color is fully transparent, then it is not hittable; see GetCurrentWorldColor().
Gets the first down and the last up touch events to the screen, regardless of actor touch event consumption.
Stage's root layer can be used to catch unconsumed touch events.

RenderTasks
Hit testing is dependent on the camera used, which is specific to each RenderTask.

Layers
For each RenderTask, hit testing starts from the top-most layer and we go through all the layers until we have a hit or there are none left.
Before we perform a hit test within a layer, we check if all the layer's parents are visible and sensitive.
If they are not, we skip hit testing the actors in that layer altogether.
If a layer is set to consume all touch, then we do not check any layers behind this layer.

Leave State
A "Leave" state is set when the first point exits the bounds of the previous first point's hit actor (primary hit actor).
When this happens, the last primary hit actor's touch or hover signal is emitted with a "Leave" state (only if it requires leave signals); see SetLeaveRequired().

Interrupted State
If a system event occurs which interrupts the touch or hover processing, then the last primary hit actor's touch or hover signals are emitted with an "Interrupted" state.
If the last primary hit actor, or one of its parents, is no longer touchable or hoverable, then its touch or hover signals are also emitted with an "Interrupted" state.
If the consumed actor on touch-down is not the same as the consumed actor on touch-up, then touch signals are also emitted from the touch-down actor with an "Interrupted" state.
If the consumed actor on hover-start is not the same as the consumed actor on hover-finished, then hover signals are also emitted from the hover-started actor with an "Interrupted" state.

detaileddescription.para:
Signals
Signal Name
Method
touched
TouchedSignal()
hovered
HoveredSignal()
wheelEvent
WheelEventSignal()
onScene
OnSceneSignal()
offScene
OffSceneSignal()
onRelayout
OnRelayoutSignal()
layoutDirectionChanged
LayoutDirectionChangedSignal()
inheritedVisibilityChanged
InheritedVisibilityChangedSignal()
@SINCE_1_0.0
